page: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation running at 3.9 GHz. The processor architecture includes 480 MB of L3 cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-decile results in multi-core stress-ng tests, PassMark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds and large block uncached read/write speeds placing in the top ten percent. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests, also rank in the top tier, alongside static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core results falling into the middle 50% range, though Geekbench single-core scores remain in the top tier.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate vector and matrix operations. This hardware acceleration is reflected in top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language model (LLM) inference across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds range from average to strong. Given the massive DDR5 memory capacity, high memory bandwidth, and lack of local storage, this bare-metal instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs across 96 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier capabilities in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance is more moderate. Advanced instruction support includes AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which drive top-tier prompt processing speeds for LLM inference. This instance is suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale in-memory databases, and CPU-based machine learning workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs, 1536.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for large-scale database and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal platform featuring 1.5 TB of DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Top-tier multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and large-scale enterprise workloads
